NKJV.1: Not unto us, O LORD, not unto us, But to Your name give glory, Because of Your mercy, Because of Your truth.
NKJV.2: Why should the Gentiles say, “So where is their God?”
NKJV.3: But our God is in heaven; He does whatever He pleases.
NKJV.4: Their idols are silver and gold, The work of men’s hands.
NKJV.5: They have mouths, but they do not speak; Eyes they have, but they do not see;
NKJV.6: They have ears, but they do not hear; Noses they have, but they do not smell;
NKJV.7: They have hands, but they do not handle; Feet they have, but they do not walk; Nor do they mutter through their throat.
NKJV.8: Those who make them are like them; So is everyone who trusts in them.
NKJV.9: O Israel, trust in the LORD; He is their help and their shield.
NKJV.10: O house of Aaron, trust in the LORD; He is their help and their shield.
NKJV.11: You who fear the LORD, trust in the LORD; He is their help and their shield.
NKJV.12: The LORD has been mindful of us; He will bless us; He will bless the house of Israel; He will bless the house of Aaron.
NKJV.13: He will bless those who fear the LORD, Both small and great.
NKJV.14: May the LORD give you increase more and more, You and your children.
NKJV.15: May you be blessed by the LORD, Who made heaven and earth.
NKJV.16: The heaven, even the heavens, are the LORD’s; But the earth He has given to the children of men.
NKJV.17: The dead do not praise the LORD, Nor any who go down into silence.
NKJV.18: But we will bless the LORD From this time forth and forevermore. Praise the LORD!

ESV.1: Not to us, O LORD, not to us, but to your name give glory, for the sake of your steadfast love and your faithfulness!
ESV.2: Why should the nations say, “Where is their God?”
ESV.3: Our God is in the heavens; he does all that he pleases.
ESV.4: Their idols are silver and gold, the work of human hands.
ESV.5: They have mouths, but do not speak; eyes, but do not see.
ESV.6: They have ears, but do not hear; noses, but do not smell.
ESV.7: They have hands, but do not feel; feet, but do not walk; and they do not make a sound in their throat.
ESV.8: Those who make them become like them; so do all who trust in them.
ESV.9: O Israel,  trust in the LORD! He is their help and their shield.
ESV.10: O house of Aaron, trust in the LORD! He is their help and their shield.
ESV.11: You who fear the LORD, trust in the LORD! He is their help and their shield.
ESV.12: The LORD has remembered us; he will bless us; he will bless the house of Israel; he will bless the house of Aaron;
ESV.13: he will bless those who fear the LORD, both the small and the great.
ESV.14: May the LORD give you increase, you and your children!
ESV.15: May you be blessed by the LORD, who made heaven and earth!
ESV.16: The heavens are the LORD's heavens, but the earth he has given to the children of man.
ESV.17: The dead do not praise the LORD, nor do any who go down into silence.
ESV.18: But we will bless the LORD from this time forth and forevermore. Praise the LORD!

NASB.1: Not to us, O LORD, not to us, But to Your name give glory Because of Your lovingkindness, because of Your truth.
NASB.2: Why should the nations say, 'Where, now, is their God?'
NASB.3: But our God is in the heavens; He does whatever He pleases.
NASB.4: Their idols are silver and gold, The work of man's hands.
NASB.5: They have mouths, but they cannot speak; They have eyes, but they cannot see;
NASB.6: They have ears, but they cannot hear; They have noses, but they cannot smell;
NASB.7: They have hands, but they cannot feel; They have feet, but they cannot walk; They cannot make a sound with their throat.
NASB.8: Those who make them will become like them, Everyone who trusts in them.
NASB.9: O Israel, trust in the LORD; He is their help and their shield.
NASB.10: O house of Aaron, trust in the LORD; He is their help and their shield.
NASB.11: You who fear the LORD, trust in the LORD; He is their help and their shield.
NASB.12: The LORD has been mindful of us; He will bless [us]; He will bless the house of Israel; He will bless the house of Aaron.
NASB.13: He will bless those who fear the LORD, The small together with the great.
NASB.14: May the LORD give you increase, You and your children.
NASB.15: May you be blessed of the LORD, Maker of heaven and earth.
NASB.16: The heavens are the heavens of the LORD, But the earth He has given to the sons of men.
NASB.17: The dead do not praise the LORD, Nor [do] any who go down into silence;
NASB.18: But as for us, we will bless the LORD From this time forth and forever. Praise the LORD!

NLT.1:
Not to us, O LORD, not to us,
but to your name goes all the glory
for your unfailing love and faithfulness.
NLT.2:
Why let the nations say,
“Where is their God?”
NLT.3:
Our God is in the heavens,
and he does as he wishes.
NLT.4:
Their idols are merely things of silver and gold,
shaped by human hands.
NLT.5:
They have mouths but cannot speak,
and eyes but cannot see.
NLT.6:
They have ears but cannot hear,
and noses but cannot smell.
NLT.7:
They have hands but cannot feel,
and feet but cannot walk,
and throats but cannot make a sound.
NLT.8:
And those who make idols are just like them,
as are all who trust in them.
NLT.9:
O Israel, trust the LORD!
He is your helper and your shield.
NLT.10:
O priests, descendants of Aaron, trust the LORD!
He is your helper and your shield.
NLT.11:
All you who fear the LORD, trust the LORD!
He is your helper and your shield.
NLT.12:
The LORD remembers us and will bless us.
He will bless the people of Israel
and bless the priests, the descendants of Aaron.
NLT.13:
He will bless those who fear the LORD,
both great and lowly.
NLT.14:
May the LORD richly bless
both you and your children.
NLT.15:
May you be blessed by the LORD,
who made heaven and earth.
NLT.16:
The heavens belong to the LORD,
but he has given the earth to all humanity.
NLT.17:
The dead cannot sing praises to the LORD,
for they have gone into the silence of the grave.
NLT.18:
But we can praise the LORD
both now and forever!
Praise the LORD!

WEB.1: Not to us, Yahweh, not to us, but to your name give glory, for your loving kindness, and for your truth’s sake.
WEB.2: Why should the nations say, “Where is their God, now?”
WEB.3: But our God is in the heavens. He does whatever he pleases.
WEB.4: Their idols are silver and gold, the work of men’s hands.
WEB.5: They have mouths, but they don’t speak. They have eyes, but they don’t see.
WEB.6: They have ears, but they don’t hear. They have noses, but they don’t smell.
WEB.7: They have hands, but they don’t feel. They have feet, but they don’t walk, neither do they speak through their throat.
WEB.8: Those who make them will be like them; yes, everyone who trusts in them.
WEB.9: Israel, trust in Yahweh! He is their help and their shield.
WEB.10: House of Aaron, trust in Yahweh! He is their help and their shield.
WEB.11: You who fear Yahweh, trust in Yahweh! He is their help and their shield.
WEB.12: Yahweh remembers us. He will bless us. He will bless the house of Israel. He will bless the house of Aaron.
WEB.13: He will bless those who fear Yahweh, both small and great.
WEB.14: May Yahweh increase you more and more, you and your children.
WEB.15: Blessed are you by Yahweh, who made heaven and earth.
WEB.16: The heavens are the heavens of Yahweh; but the earth has he given to the children of men.
WEB.17: The dead don’t praise Yah, neither any who go down into silence;
WEB.18: But we will bless Yah, from this time forward and forever more. Praise Yah!

CSB17.1:
Not to us, LORD, not to us,
but to your name give glory
because of your faithful love, because of your truth. ⓕ
CSB17.2:
Why should the nations say,
“Where is their God?” ⓖ
CSB17.3:
Our God is in heaven
and does whatever he pleases. ⓗ
CSB17.4:
Their idols are silver and gold,
made by human hands. ⓘ
CSB17.5:
They have mouths but cannot speak,
eyes, but cannot see.
CSB17.6:
They have ears but cannot hear,
noses, but cannot smell. ⓙ
CSB17.7:
They have hands but cannot feel,
feet, but cannot walk. ⓚ
They cannot make a sound with their throats. ⓛ
CSB17.8:
Those who make them are [1] just like them,
as are all who trust in them. ⓜ
CSB17.9:
Israel, [2] trust in the LORD! ⓝ
He is their help and shield. ⓐ
CSB17.10:
House of Aaron, trust in the LORD! ⓑ
He is their help and shield. ⓒ
CSB17.11:
You who fear the LORD, trust in the LORD! ⓓ
He is their help and shield.
CSB17.12:
The LORD remembers us and will bless us.
He will bless the house of Israel;
he will bless the house of Aaron; ⓔ
CSB17.13:
he will bless those who fear the LORD  —
small and great alike. ⓕ
CSB17.14:
May the LORD add to your numbers,
both yours and your children’s. ⓖ
CSB17.15:
May you be blessed by the LORD,
the Maker of heaven and earth. ⓗ
CSB17.16:
The heavens are the LORD’s, [3]
but the earth he has given to the human race. ⓘ
CSB17.17:
It is not the dead who praise the LORD,
nor any of those descending into the silence of death. ⓙ
CSB17.18:
But we will bless the LORD,
both now and forever. ⓚ
Hallelujah!

AMP.1:
Not to us, O LORD, not to us,
But to Your name give glory
Because of Your lovingkindness, because of Your truth and faithfulness.
AMP.2:
Why should the nations say,
"Where, now, is their God?"
AMP.3:
But our God is in heaven;
He does whatever He pleases.
AMP.4:
The idols [of the nations] are silver and gold,
The work of man's hands.
AMP.5:
They have mouths, but they cannot speak;
They have eyes, but they cannot see;
AMP.6:
They have ears, but they cannot hear;
They have noses, but they cannot smell;
AMP.7:
They have hands, but they cannot feel;
They have feet, but they cannot walk;
Nor can they make a sound with their throats.
AMP.8:
Those who make them will become like them,
Everyone who trusts in them.
AMP.9:
O Israel, trust and take refuge in the LORD! [Be confident in Him, cling to Him, rely on His word!]
He is their help and their shield.
AMP.10:
O house of Aaron, trust in the LORD;
He is their help and their shield.
AMP.11:
You who [reverently] fear the LORD, trust in LORD;
He is their help and their shield.
AMP.12:
The LORD has been mindful of us; He will bless,
He will bless the house of Israel;
He will bless the house of Aaron.
AMP.13:
He will bless those who fear and worship the LORD [with awe-inspired reverence and submissive wonder],
Both the small and the great.
AMP.14:
May the LORD give you [great] increase,
You and your children.
AMP.15:
May you be blessed of the LORD,
Who made heaven and earth.
AMP.16:
The heavens are the heavens of the LORD,
But the earth He has given to the children of men.
AMP.17:
The dead do not praise the LORD,
Nor do any who go down into silence;
AMP.18:
But as for us, we will bless and affectionately and gratefully praise the LORD
From this time forth and forever.
Praise the LORD! (Hallelujah!)

MSG.1:
Not for our sake, God, no, not for our sake,
but for your name's sake, show your glory.
Do it on account of your merciful love,
do it on account of your faithful ways.
MSG.2:
Do it so none of the nations can say,
"Where now, oh where is their God?"
MSG.3:
Our God is in heaven
doing whatever he wants to do.
MSG.4:
Their gods are metal and wood,
handmade in a basement shop:
MSG.5:
Carved mouths that can't talk,
painted eyes that can't see,
MSG.6:
Tin ears that can't hear,
molded noses that can't smell,
MSG.7:
Hands that can't grasp, feet that can't walk or run,
throats that never utter a sound.
MSG.8:
Those who make them have become just like them,
have become just like the gods they trust.
MSG.9:
But you, Israel: put your trust in God!
—trust your Helper! trust your Ruler!
MSG.10:
Clan of Aaron, trust in God!
—trust your Helper! trust your Ruler!
MSG.11:
You who fear God, trust in God!
—trust your Helper! trust your Ruler!
MSG.12:
O God, remember us and bless us,
bless the families of Israel and Aaron.
MSG.13:
And let God bless all who fear God—
bless the small, bless the great.
MSG.14:
Oh, let God enlarge your families—
giving growth to you, growth to your children.
MSG.15:
May you be blessed by God,
by God, who made heaven and earth.
MSG.16:
The heaven of heavens is for God,
but he put us in charge of the earth.
MSG.17:
Dead people can't praise God—
not a word to be heard from those buried in the ground.
MSG.18:
But we bless God, oh yes—
we bless him now, we bless him always!
Hallelujah!

NIV.1:
Psalm 115
Not to us, O Lord, not to us
but to your name be the glory,
because of your love and faithfulness.
NIV.2:
Why do the nations say,
“Where is their God?”
NIV.3:
Our God is in heaven;
he does whatever pleases him.
NIV.4:
But their idols are silver and gold,
made by the hands of men.
NIV.5:
They have mouths, but cannot speak,
eyes, but they cannot see;
NIV.6:
they have ears, but cannot hear,
noses, but they cannot smell;
NIV.7:
they have hands, but cannot feel,
feet, but they cannot walk;
nor can they utter a sound with their throats.
NIV.8:
Those who make them will be like them,
and so will all who trust in them.
NIV.9:
O house of Israel, trust in the Lord—
he is their help and shield.
NIV.10:
O house of Aaron, trust in the Lord—
he is their help and shield.
NIV.11:
You who fear him, trust in the Lord—
he is their help and shield.
NIV.12:
The Lord remembers us and will bless us:
He will bless the house of Israel,
he will bless the house of Aaron,
NIV.13:
he will bless those who fear the Lord—
small and great alike.
NIV.14:
May the Lord make you increase,
both you and your children.
NIV.15:
May you be blessed by the Lord,
the Maker of heaven and earth.
NIV.16:
The highest heavens belong to the Lord,
but the earth he has given to man.
NIV.17:
It is not the dead who praise the Lord,
those who go down to silence;
NIV.18:
it is we who extol the Lord,
both now and forevermore.
Praise the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
Not to us, O LORD, not to us, but to your name give glory,
for the sake of your steadfast love and your faithfulness.
NRSVCE.2:
Why should the nations say,
“Where is their God?”
NRSVCE.3:
Our God is in the heavens;
he does whatever he pleases.
NRSVCE.4:
Their idols are silver and gold,
the work of human hands.
NRSVCE.5:
They have mouths, but do not speak;
eyes, but do not see.
NRSVCE.6:
They have ears, but do not hear;
noses, but do not smell.
NRSVCE.7:
They have hands, but do not feel;
feet, but do not walk;
they make no sound in their throats.
NRSVCE.8:
Those who make them are like them;
so are all who trust in them.
NRSVCE.9:
O Israel, trust in the LORD!
He is their help and their shield.
NRSVCE.10:
O house of Aaron, trust in the LORD!
He is their help and their shield.
NRSVCE.11:
You who fear the LORD, trust in the LORD!
He is their help and their shield.
NRSVCE.12:
The LORD has been mindful of us; he will bless us;
he will bless the house of Israel;
he will bless the house of Aaron;
NRSVCE.13:
he will bless those who fear the LORD,
both small and great.
NRSVCE.14:
May the LORD give you increase,
both you and your children.
NRSVCE.15:
May you be blessed by the LORD,
who made heaven and earth.
NRSVCE.16:
The heavens are the LORD’s heavens,
but the earth he has given to human beings.
NRSVCE.17:
The dead do not praise the LORD,
nor do any that go down into silence.
NRSVCE.18:
But we will bless the LORD
from this time on and forevermore.
Praise the LORD!

NET.1: Not to us, O LORD, not to us! But to your name bring honor, for the sake of your loyal love and faithfulness.
NET.2: Why should the nations say, “Where is their God?”
NET.3: Our God is in heaven! He does whatever he pleases!
NET.4: Their idols are made of silver and gold — they are man-made.
NET.5: They have mouths, but cannot speak, eyes, but cannot see,
NET.6: ears, but cannot hear, noses, but cannot smell,
NET.7: hands, but cannot touch, feet, but cannot walk. They cannot even clear their throats.
NET.8: Those who make them will end up like them, as will everyone who trusts in them.
NET.9: O Israel, trust in the LORD! He is their deliverer and protector.
NET.10: O family of Aaron, trust in the LORD! He is their deliverer and protector.
NET.11: You loyal followers of the LORD, trust in the LORD! He is their deliverer and protector.
NET.12: The LORD takes notice of us, he will bless — he will bless the family of Israel, he will bless the family of Aaron.
NET.13: He will bless his loyal followers, both young and old.
NET.14: May he increase your numbers, yours and your children’s!
NET.15: May you be blessed by the LORD, the creator of heaven and earth!
NET.16: The heavens belong to the LORD, but the earth he has given to mankind.
NET.17: The dead do not praise the LORD, nor do any of those who descend into the silence of death.
NET.18: But we will praise the LORD now and forevermore. Praise the LORD!
